items.title: Expectation and variance
items.description: Cov(X, Y) is defined from expectations: E[(X - μ_X)(Y - μ_Y)] = E[XY] - E[X]E[Y]. Var(X) is the special case Cov(X, X).

items.title: Matrices and operations
items.description: The covariance matrix is a symmetric matrix built from outer products of centered columns; matrix multiplication computes C = X̃ᵀ X̃ / (n - 1).

items.title: Principal Component Analysis
items.description: PCA eigendecomposes the covariance matrix (or SVD of centered data) to find orthogonal directions ranked by variance (eigenvalues).

items.title: Scaling and normalization
items.description: Standardizing features (zero mean, unit variance) turns a covariance matrix into a correlation matrix; preprocessing choices change covariance but not Pearson r between variables.
